title: Nokia launches AI networking lab to drive co-innovation with partners and accelerate next era of AI-native data center networking
author: Nokia
description: Nokia has launched an AI Networking Innovation Lab in Sunnyvale, California to develop next-generation data center networking technologies for AI infrastructure. The lab serves as a co-innovation hub with partners including AMD, Keysight, Lenovo, Supermicro, and Weka, focusing on testing and validating AI-native networking solutions through three pillars: technology innovation, ecosystem collaboration, and validation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: AMD is collaborating with Nokia to co-develop and test AI solutions, ensuring their enterprise AI solutions are validated with real-world workloads, which enhances product credibility and market positioning.
Keysight is benchmarking and optimizing AI networks in the lab, gaining validated insights that help accelerate AI network adoption and provide competitive advantages in network testing solutions.
Lenovo is mentioned as a partner but no specific details about their involvement or benefits are provided in the article.
Supermicro is listed as a technology partner but no specific details about their collaboration or involvement are provided in the article.

Nokia is launching a strategic innovation lab to strengthen its position in AI-native networking, demonstrating commitment to next-generation infrastructure and positioning itself as a leader in AI-driven connectivity.

title: Why Is Keysight Stock Gaining Wednesday?
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Keysight Technologies reported strong second-quarter earnings of $2.87 per share, beating analyst estimates of $2.32, with revenue rising 31% year-over-year to $1.72 billion. The company raised its full-year fiscal 2026 outlook, expecting high-20% revenue growth. Strong demand in AI-related businesses, semiconductor testing, and aerospace/defense markets drove performance. The stock gained 2.29% in premarket trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company significantly beat earnings expectations ($2.87 vs $2.32 consensus), delivered 31% revenue growth, raised full-year guidance to high-20% range, reported record free cash flow of $472 million, and demonstrated strong momentum in high-growth AI and semiconductor markets with robust customer pipeline visibility.

title: Keysight Stock Climbs On Record Q2 Earnings Beat: Details
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Keysight Technologies reported record Q2 earnings of $2.87 per share, beating analyst estimates by 23.71%, with revenue of $1.72 billion slightly exceeding expectations. The company raised fiscal 2026 guidance and expects Q3 EPS of $2.43-$2.49 and revenue of $1.73-$1.75 billion, both above analyst estimates. KEYS stock climbed 8.63% in after-hours trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company delivered record quarterly results with significant EPS beat (23.71% above consensus), revenue growth year-over-year, raised full-year guidance, and demonstrated strong momentum with all-time highs in orders, revenue, and free cash flow. Forward guidance also exceeded analyst expectations.

title: The AI Supercycle Just Entered Phase 2. Most Investors Aren't Ready.
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: The article argues that AI investment is shifting from the flashy chip and hyperscaler phase to a quieter 'Phase 2' focused on infrastructure validation and rural broadband deployment. Two companies positioned to benefit are Keysight Technologies, which validates data center hardware, and Calix, which provides AI platforms for rural broadband providers leveraging federal BEAD funding.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company reported 23% YoY revenue growth, record orders of $1.65B, and 80% stock gain over 52 weeks. Strong demand for optical transceiver validation driven by hyperscaler AI buildouts. Trading 10% below analyst consensus target with recurring revenue at 29% providing business stickiness.

Demonstrated strong execution with record Q3 2025 revenue of $265M, five consecutive quarters of sequential growth, and seven consecutive quarters of gross margin improvement. One-third of 1,200 customers already adopted new Calix One platform. Winning BEAD contracts positions company to benefit from billions in federal rural fiber funding.
Mentioned as part of Phase 1 of AI supercycle (chips and hyperscalers). Article suggests easy money in this phase has already been made, implying limited upside relative to Phase 2 opportunities.
Mentioned as the cloud provider (Google Cloud) underlying Calix One platform. No direct investment thesis presented; mentioned only as infrastructure partner.
Mentioned as the cloud provider (Google Cloud) underlying Calix One platform. No direct investment thesis presented; mentioned only as infrastructure partner.

title: At 6G@UT Forum, InterDigital to Demonstrate AI-enabled Teleoperation and Energy Efficient Edge Intelligence
author: Interdigital, Inc.
description: InterDigital will demonstrate AI-enhanced services and energy-efficient edge intelligence at the 6G@UT Forum at University of Texas at Austin on April 9th. The company will showcase multi-user MIMO over-the-air federated learning technology and AI-enabled teleoperation for remote vehicle operation over cellular networks, in partnership with UT Austin and Keysight Technologies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is demonstrating significant technological advancements in 6G, AI, and edge computing. Leadership participation in industry forums and partnerships with major institutions (UT Austin, Keysight) demonstrate strong market positioning and innovation leadership in emerging wireless technologies.

Mentioned as a research partner on federated learning project. Partnership indicates involvement in cutting-edge 6G research but no direct business impact or sentiment drivers are evident from the article.
